scope: FQ-02: background trees and pass-through flora — the player walks past most trees instead of jumping over or chopping through every one.
summary: Tree generation on the existing tree seed channel now splits each accepted site into a solid foreground wood column (3-5 tall, unchanged mining/drops/axe behavior) or a pass-through background tree (4-7 bg_trunk column + 3-wide bg_canopy). WorldGen.generate returns a separate background dictionary; world.gd renders it on a new BackgroundFlora TileMapLayer added before the Blocks layer, modulated with a dim cool tint, whose tileset has zero physics and zero occlusion layers — background flora can never collide, block light, or count as shelter. New config key generation.tree_foreground_ratio (0-1, default 0.4) with a 'Solid Tree Ratio' world-builder slider and gen_help text; a foreground tree is forced after 2 consecutive background trees so wood stays harvestable. Background cells are deterministic from seed+config: never serialized, never in cells/deltas, never mineable or placeable, y>=0 bounds-guarded, cleared across the Town Hall footprint columns; bush generation skips background-occupied cells. blocks.json unchanged (bg ids are not blocks). Smoke grew 134 -> 142 with a live traversal test walking the player past a background trunk on flat terrain using only move_right.
acceptance: Surface generation creates pass-through background trees (70 background cells + 30 foreground wood on default config, seed 777)
Player movement is not blocked by background trees (live walk-past check x=2231.0 past target 2168.0; background tileset has zero physics layers)
Foreground wood still exists, mines, drops wood, and keeps axe behavior (wood 33 frames, 24 with axe — unchanged v0.6 baselines)
Berry bush support/regrowth unbroken (all wave_e_* checks green; bushes never spawn on background cells)
tree_density and tree_foreground_ratio affect the generated surfaces (density 0 clears both layers; ratio 0 -> all background, ratio 1 -> all foreground)
Smoke covers the foreground/background distinction: 8 fq02_* checks, 142/142 PASS on windowed Godot 4.6.1 at 2026-07-07T10:34; validator PASS; capsule doctor healthy; git diff --check clean
No unrelated feature work: diff limited to world_gen/world/smoke/world_settings + one shell slider row + docs
validation: COHERONIA_SMOKE=1 waited windowed run -> 142/142 PASS incl. eight fq02_* checks; fresh results file verified by LastWriteTime
decisions: Background trees are pure visuals owned by world_gen/world, not entries in blocks.json or cells — mining, placement, collision, save/load, and settlement scoring are untouched by construction
Background flora is regenerated from seed+config and never serialized; no save schema change
Harvesting background trees deliberately not built (queue scope said not to overbuild); revisit only if a clear-flora action is wanted
tree_foreground_ratio defaults to 0.4 with a forced foreground tree after 2 consecutive background trees, keeping wood supply meaningful at any ratio > 0
Review findings fixed pre-commit: y>=0 bounds guard on background stamping; vacuous is_solid_at smoke sub-check replaced by an in-bounds + no-overlap assertion
Review finding rejected with rationale: bush regrowth need not consult background_cells because regrowth only targets former bush cells, which generation guarantees are never background cells

Orchestration: Explore recon agent -> orchestrator implementation -> Windows-side verification -> sonnet review agent -> orchestrator fixes -> full re-verification (first review agent launch died on a session-token limit and was relaunched after reset)
risks: Pre-FQ-02 world saves regenerate with some former solid trees now background flora; deltas still apply cleanly — cosmetic only, no data loss
Background art is placeholder-grade like all current visuals; FQ-07 visual asset pipeline is the upgrade path
Default ratio 0.4 feel untested by human play; tunable per world in the builder
next_action: FQ-03: equipment data model and character-owned gear slots.
